text.heroSub: A website dedicated to one of the most prominent movements in United States history.
text.whoTitle: If you want to remember me, organize.
text.whoLede: Founded by César Chávez & Dolores Huerta
text.whoBody: The United Farm Workers (UFW), successor of the National Farm Workers Association (NFWA), was the organized collaboration between many Chicano activists, advocating for the defense of farmworkers through nonviolent tactics including marches, national boycotts, and fasts. As the movement gained national attention, the group were able to receive better working conditions and higher wages from newly signed contracts.
text.causeIntro: Briefly introduce what the movement was fighting for.
text.flashIntro: Introduce the major events of the movement.
text.legacyText: Summarize the movement's lasting impact here.
text.footerNote: A student research project. Every quotation, image, and fact is documented in our Annotated Bibliography (MLA).
